page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance type powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores, utilizing the AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the processor demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 3114.08, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. However, multi-core scaling is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core performance and PassMark CPU scores falling into the average range. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, including cached read-only small block speeds of 616.12 GB/sec and uncached large block read/write speeds, also align with average tier performance. Cryptographic and vector execution capabilities are supported by hardware flags such as AVX-512, AES, and SHA_NI, which assist in maintaining steady throughput for encrypted operations and database tasks.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.g8ae.2xlarge offers a balanced hardware profile for workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy operations. The instance is suited for general-purpose enterprise workloads, including web serving, database operations, and moderate multi-threaded application hosting.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via KVM, it includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Network performance is defined by a 6 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, while multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the average tier. The instance provides a balanced resource profile for general enterprise workloads, such as static web serving, Redis operations, and standard database hosting, where dedicated CPU execution is required without the need for high-speed local NVMe storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU, 32 GB RAM general-purpose instance with top-tier single-core CPU performance and KVM virtualization.
tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, balanced memory allocation, and top-tier single-core execution speeds.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
